rag_rules: Keep type-scope contract content with the component type owner and implementation-scope binding content with the component implementation owner.
When fusing implementation-scope content, preserve ordering so it appears after the structural declarations it depends on.
Use the input AADL model as the authority for valid owners, names, ports, subcomponents, connections, and properties.
generic_rules: Insert generated annex blocks without changing existing architecture, declarations, connections, or properties outside the intended owner.
Match each generated block to the exact owner kind and scope it was produced for; do not shift content between type and implementation levels.
Preserve existing text and identifiers verbatim unless the generated block itself requires new annex text.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped as an `annex agree {** ... **};` block attached to a valid AADL owner.
anti_patterns: Do not infer owners, identifiers, or scope from requirement wording when the AADL model does not confirm them.
Do not move implementation-specific references into a type owner or place type-level contract content into an implementation owner.
Do not rewrite, delete, or reorder structural model elements as part of annex insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Boolean or opaque data range repair rules
AADL property names as AGREE expression variables
